mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives S&P 500 forward returns higher over a 5-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO level change vs forward 5-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above the 1.0% threshold, it signals the start of monetary tightening which increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After trigger activation, positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50% of instances, or mean return is not significantly negative at p<0.05 over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) validates consumer-driven economic momentum, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, pushing S&P 500 forward returns higher over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% and PAYEMS growth > 0.1% for two consecutive months
failure_condition: When joint growth conditions are met, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of occurrences over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong economic expansion (GDP growth > 2.5% annualized), rising housing starts signal sustained consumer demand and residential investment, which boosts economic momentum and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: strong economic expansion (GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth > 2.5%)
failure_condition: In the expansion regime, HOUST month-over-month increases > 2% are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate indicates tightening labor market conditions associated with higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings, positively influencing S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: When UNRATE declines by 0.2% or more over a month, subsequent 60-day returns are negative in more than half of such events over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth exceeds 3.0% annualized, it signals above-trend economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in industrial production and retail sales indicates synchronized expansion across production and consumption, reinforcing corporate earnings momentum and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both INDPRO and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: When both conditions hold, S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in at least 30% of occurrences over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a low federal funds rate environment (below 1.0%), an increase in FEDFUNDS signifies a normalization of monetary policy from emergency levels, restoring investor confidence in economic stability and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1.0% and rises by any amount, forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year period.
